name: Axiom Event Data Platform API
what_it_does: Event data platform with REST API for ingesting, storing, and querying any structured event data at scale — optimized for serverless and edge environments with OpenTelemetry support, Datadog-compatible API, and cost-effective log storage.
use_cases: Ingesting application logs and events via REST API or OpenTelemetry at any scale
Querying structured event data with APL (Axiom Processing Language) for analytics
Serverless and edge observability where traditional agents can't run
Cost-effective alternative to Datadog or Splunk for log storage and querying
Building analytics and monitoring workflows on event data via REST and APL queries
not_for: Infrastructure metrics collection with Prometheus-style scraping (Axiom is event-ingestion focused)
Teams needing on-premise data sovereignty without cloud dependencies
Real-time dashboards requiring sub-second query latency for live streaming
best_when: A team needs cost-effective event data storage and querying for serverless or edge applications where traditional observability tools are too expensive or complex.
avoid_when: You need on-premise deployment, Prometheus metrics scraping, or complex alerting rule management.

auth.notes: API token from Axiom dashboard. Bearer token authentication. Dataset-scoped tokens available for least-privilege access. Ingest-only tokens for data sources. Management tokens for full access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $25/month; Team from $75; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Extremely generous free tier — 500GB ingest is substantial. Very competitive pricing vs. Datadog and Splunk. Popular with Vercel, Cloudflare Workers, and serverless developers. OpenTelemetry native.

performance.rate_limits: Ingest rate limits per plan; query rate limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for dataset management, ingest, and querying via APL. Multi-language SDKs. OpenTelemetry native ingestion. Datadog-compatible API for easy migration. APL is powerful but has learning curve.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Event ingestion creates new records — not idempotent. Query operations are idempotent reads. Dataset management operations are idempotent for same parameters.
known_agent_gotchas: APL (Axiom Processing Language) is Kusto-inspired — learning curve for SQL-familiar agents
Event schema is flexible but query requires knowing field names
Ingest-only vs. full tokens — use ingest-only tokens for data sources
Query results may return large datasets — implement cursor pagination for large result sets

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Scoped API tokens (ingest-only vs. full access). SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. Token granularity reduces blast radius of credential compromise.
